title: Creating a table with duplicate column name should not be possible
poc: CREATE TABLE products (id int not null primary key, name varchar(99) not null, price decimal(7,2) not null);
INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (6, 'beer', 1.55);
INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (8, 'wine', 5.67);
CREATE TABLE products_new (id serial, like products);
SELECT id, id, name, price from products_new;
SELECT id, name, price from products_new;
INSERT INTO products_new (id, name, price) VALUES (6, 'beer', 1.55);
INSERT INTO products_new (id, name, price) VALUES (8, 'wine', 5.67);
CREATE TABLE products_new2 (id serial, like products, name2 varchar(299) not null, like products);
INSERT INTO products_new2 (id, name2, price) VALUES (6, 'beer', 1.55);
INSERT INTO products_new2 (id, name2, price) VALUES (8, 'wine', 5.67);
key_bug_features: Creating tables with duplicate column names allowed
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (id int not null primary key, name varchar(99) not null, price decimal(7,2) not null);
CREATE TABLE products_duplicate (id int not null primary key, name varchar(99) not null, name varchar(99) not null, price decimal(7,2) not null);
INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (1, 'apple', 0.99);
INSERT INTO products_duplicate (id, name, price) VALUES (2, 'orange', 1.29);
SELECT id, name, price FROM products_duplicate;
